RADIOACTIVE MAN by John Swartzwelder ACT ONE FADE IN: SCENE | EXT. COMIC BOOK SHOP - ESTABLISHING <a INT. COMIC BOOK SHOP BART and MILHOUSE are scornfully looking through a rack of newly released comics. We can see, among others, "Cat Girl," and "Bat Boy." We also see a copy of "Man Boy," but it looks like it might be in the wrong rack. BART All these new superheroes suck. None of them can hold a candle to Radioactive Man. MILHOUSE The only decent new one is Radiation Dude. BART (SNORTS) He’s just a cheap imitation of Radioactive Man. MILHOUSE

Explain.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 2. SCENE _| Cont BART The similarities are subtle, but many. For example, Radioactive Man has his famous catchphrase "Up and Aton" (EXPLAINING) - with atom spelled a-t-o- m in a delicious pun... MILHOUSE (NODDING) Go on. BART «+. while Radiation Dude has a similar, but lamer catchphrase: "Up and Let‘’s Go." The COMIC BOOK GUY has been listening to all this. COMIC BOOK GUY You kids fancy yourselves experts, eh? BART (COCKILY) Well, between us we’ve read all 814 issues of Radioactive Man. MILHOUSE And both of us have the special limited edition issue where he and Fallout Boy get killed on every page. COMIC BOOK GUY I suppose you know Hollywood is planning a feature film about "Radioactive Man."

Both Bart and Milhouse’s caps fly straight up and out of frame.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 3. SCENE | ConT'D et ee COMIC BOOK GUY (CONT'D) I gotta do something about that air conditioner suction.

We see the caps are quivering against the grill of a ceiling vent.

BART (EAGERLY) Who’s going to play Radioactive Man? COMIC BOOK GUY I will tell you in exactly seven minutes. He goes in the back. INT. BACK OF COMIC BOOK STORE The comic book guy sits at his computer, turns it on, and taps his foot impatiently as DOS is loaded and several dozen other routines are run through. Finally the words "Code Name?" appear. COMIC BOOK GUY (TYPING) Code name... "Quadruple-oh- seven." The computer goes through several other routines. COMIC BOOK GUY (CONT’D) (AS HE TYPES) "Need know star RM pic." INT. SOME NERD’S HOUSE A NERD at a computer reads the comic book guy’s message, puts on a pair of Spock ears, and begins TYPING rapidly. He shrinks to a smaller square on the screen and four OTHER

NERDS appear in boxes passing along the message. The last nerd is PRINCE.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 4. SCENE | CoNT'D

INT. HOLLYWOOD STUDIO - CONFERENCE ROOM — Finally, a NERD is crouched under the conference room table. He reads the comic book guy’s question on his laptop computer, then puts a listening device on the underside of the table. NEW ANGLE

We see the BOARD OF DIRECTORS of the film company, as well as the PRODUCER and DIRECTOR of the film.

OLD BOARD MEMBER I don’t see why Rainier Wolfcastle should be the star. I think we should bring back Dirk Richter. Kids will want to see the original Radioactive Man.

PRODUCER (PATIENTLY) I keep telling you. He’s 73 years old and he’s DEAD.

OLD BOARD MEMBER Granted, but...

DIRECTOR Besides, we want to stay as far away from the campy 70’s version as possible.

Everyone looks at the large 1973 Radioactive Man movie poster on the wall.

CLIP FROM 70’S RADIOACTIVE MAN MOVIE RADIOACTIVE MAN and FALLOUT BOY are in an abandoned

warehouse. Out of the shadows comes a sixty-year-old PAUL LYNDE-TYPE in a boy scout uniform. Fallout Boy sees hin.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 5. c SCENE | Cont'd FALLOUT BOY a Billowing backpacks, Radioactive Man! It’s the worst villain of them all: The Scoutmaster! RADIOACTIVE MAN (DIRK RICHTER) (GRIMLY) I see him, Fallout Boy. SCOUTMASTER (A LA PAUL LYNDE) Go get ‘em, scouts! A bunch of 50ish HENCHMEN in boy scout uniforms surround our heros. One makes a fire by the Indian bow method encircling Radioacitve Man and Fallout Boy. Everyone begins FIGHTING. Batman style "Pow" words appear including “Zuff," "Pan," "Snuh," "Bart," "Poo," "Mint" and "Zak." SCOUTMASTER (CONT’D) Don’t be afraid to use your nails, boys! After all the henchmen are knocked out, Radioactive Man opens a freezer, freeing a dozen Go-Go DANCERS, who immediately begin dancing. Radioactive Man and Fallout Boy begin go-go dancing too. After a moment, the bad guys recover and half~heartedly join in. BACK TO SCENE

The producer holds up a map of the U.S. that has question marks drawn on it.

PRODUCER Let’s talk locations. We need a city that has a nuclear reactor and a gorge, and can guarantee us the full cooperation of city officials.

OLD BOARD MEMBER

How about Pompeii?

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 6.

SCENE | Cont'd

ASSISTANT I’1l check "Variety."

They begin thumbing through "Variety." We see huge, full- color scenic ads for New York, Utah, and Texas.

PRODUCER Wow! Look at that ad!

We see he’s looking at a tiny black and white ad that says, "Flim Springfield."

PRODUCER (CONT‘D) This place must be hot. They don’t need a big ad or even correct spelling. OLD BOARD MEMBER I agree with that logic. PRODUCER (INTO INTERCOM) Get me two plane tickets to the state that Springfield is in. INT. CITY HALL - QUIMBY’S OFFICE Scene 2

QUIMBY and WIGGUM are eagerly leaning over the producer and the director, who both look uncomfortable.

PRODUCER All right. We have 30 million dollars to spend. We’1ll make the movie here. QUIMBY We'll blow up our dams, destroy forests, anything. If there’s a species of animal that’s causing you problems - nosing around your camera -

we'll have it wiped out.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 7.

SCENE d Cott D

PRODUCER (A LITTLE ALARMED) Well don’t do that. WIGGUM You won’t need stuntmen. We have condemned criminals you can use. Drop a ton of water on ’em, push ‘em off cliffs, we don’t care. DIRECTOR Look, we just want to make movies. Not kill things. WIGGUM (CONSPIRATORIALLY; STILTED) Right. We understand. He gives them a big wink. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - BART’S CLASS An announcement comes over the P.A. SKINNER (0.S.) (OVER P.A.) Students, I have an announcement. One of your favorite comic book heroes, Radio Man... NELSON

(YELLING) Radioactive Man, stupid.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 8.

SCENE 2 ColT'D

SKINNER (0.S.) (OVER P.A.) Strange. I shouldn’t have been able to hear that. Anyway, a Hollywood studio has decided to film the Radioactive Man movie here in Springfield. KIDS Yayyyyyy! SKINNER (0.S.) (OVER P.A.) And they will be holding auditions to find a local youngster to play Fallout Boy. Many of the CHILDREN’S hats fly up and out of frame. SKINNER (0.S.) (CONT’D) Oh, and the air conditioner will be fixed this afternoon. CU - NEWSPAPER The headline is: "Who Will Be Fallout Boy?" Subhead says: "Who Will Be Fallout Boy?" (We see a silhouette of a boy with a question mark). INT. SIMPSON HOME The FAMILY is eating dinner. Bart stands on a counter, looking at a comic book briefly, then begins acting out a line. BART Look behind you, Radioactive Man! The sun is exploding again!

(Bart dramatically dives off of the counter onto the floor).

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 9.

SCAIE 2 CNTID

MARGE Bart, why are you talking like that? HOMER Yeah. And who in the hell are you talking to? Marge, do you have other men in this house? Radioactive men? BART I’m practicing my Fallout Boy dialogue. If I get this role, I could finally come to terms with this funny little muddle called Bart. MARGE I don’t know if this is such a good idea. So many child stars become criminals. BART Let’s face it, I’m headed that way anyway, Mom. This lets you off the hook. MARGE (INTRIGUED) Hmmm... true. HOMER (STERNLY) You haven’t answered my question, Marge. And now I’ve forgotten it. MONTAGE

A) Banners are put up on buildings and across streets that say "Welcome Hollywood Money."

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 10.

SLENE 2 aad B) We see Homer watching TV. He CLICKS rapidly through a number of TV channels. We see a quick series of different announcers. DIFFERENT ANNOUNCERS (ONE SYLLABLE EACH) Ev - er - y - one - is - talk - king - a - bout - Ra - di - o - ac - tive - Ma - an - y’all. INT. MOE’S MOE is holding up a tattered movie poster for a show called "Laff And A Haff." It shows the Little Rascals, with a young Moe in the center of the group. BARNEY Wow! You mean you were one of the original Little Rascals? MOE Yeah. HOMER Which one were you? The ugly one? Moe looks at him for a beat. HOMER (CONT’D)

Were you the ugly one?

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 11.

SCENE 2 GNTD

MOE No. I was the tough kid. Smelly. My shtick was looking into an exhaust pipe and getting a faceful of soot. Nobody could do that better than me. Of course, it was kinda hard to think of reasons for me to look in that exhaust pipe every time, but we had good writers, William Faulkner could write an exhaust pipe gag that would really make you think. BARNEY If you were such a big shot, why aren’t you still making movies? Moe gets a haunted look. BARNEY (CONT’D) Moe?... Moe? MOE’S MEMORY The Little Rascals are playing marbles. Moe, as "Smelly," FIRES a marble into the circle. It bounces and goes into an exhaust pipe. ALFALFA peers into the exhaust pipe and gets a face full of soot. Moe grabs Alfalfa’s head and begins smashing it against the pavement. MOE (AS SMELLY) You stole my bit! That’s my bit! A stagehand runs in. STAGEHAND Oh my God! He’s killed the original

Alfalfa!

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 12.

SCENE 2 CONT'D

MOE (AS SMELLY) (OMINOUSLY) You’re next, Faulkner.

We see WILLIAM FAULKNER in a canvas chair nervously take a swig from a flask.

BACK TO SCENE Moe looks troubled. MOE Luckily, Alfalfa was an orphan owned by the studio. ALL (MURMUR UNDERSTANDING ASSENT) ON TV KENT BROCKMAN With wealthy Hollywood people on the way, local merchants can be forgiven for raising their prices a little. We see news footage of SHOPOWNERS on Main Street adding zeros to prices that are posted in their windows. A

parking lot sign now says "All-Day Parking: A Zillion Dollars."

INT. KWIK-E-MART SCENE 3 APU is glaring at SANJAY. SANJAY But Apu... APU No. It is unethical to charge more than our normal 80% mark-up. You know it, I know it, and many of our gods

know it.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page

SCENE 3 cont

SANJAY

Yes, but... APU Yes but nothing! 90%! That’s it! EXT. CITY LIMITS

EVERYONE in town is waiting by the road at the city limits sign.

GRAMPA Here they come! A stream of MOVIE PEOPLE in cars, vans and trucks DRIVE past. The townspeople watch them disappear towards Springfield, then they look at Grampa. GRAMPA (CONT/D) That was them! The townspeople begin running towards town. EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - ESTABLISHING

A sign says, "Fallout Boy Auditions Here. Classes Suspended Until Further Notice."

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - HALLWAY It is full of KIDS practicing their lines. ALL KIDS (VARIOUS BAD READINGS) Watch out, Radioactive Man. BART Watch out! All the kids GASP and duck and turn in fear. BART (CONT’D) (FINISHING) ...Radioactive Man!!!

The kids realize that Bart was simply acting and APPLAUD vigorously.

13.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 14. ENE 3 ONT'D

BART (CONT’D) <a It’s all in the delivery. (EMOTING) .-.Now is the winter of our discontent...

Again the kids GASP and duck.

RALPH

Oh no! Run!

The kids then realize Bart was acting and again break into vigorous APPLAUSE.

ANGLE ON MILHOUSE AND MRS. VAN HOUTEN MILHOUSE Mom, don’t make me do this. I don’t want to be an actor. I want a career in fasteners, all kinds of fasteners. (WITH DISTASTE) Except clasps. No clasps. MRS. VAN HOUTEN Nonsense. You’re going to be great. Now take off those glasses so they can see your beautiful eyes. She removes his glasses, revealing beady pig eyes. ANGLE ON JIMBO JIMBO Hey, help me rehearse. Does Fallout Boy punch like this? (PUNCHES MARTIN) Or is it more like this? (DIFFERENT PUNCH; THEN DISGUSTED) Oh, forget it.

He starts to walk away.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 15.

SCENE 2 ofi'D

MARTIN Jimbo, quitters never win. And winners never quit. Persevere. Persevere. JIMBO (MOVED) You’re right. Thanks. He begins beating Martin again. MARTIN (IN SOME PAIN) That’s the spirit. INT. SKINNER’S OFFICE The director, producer, a CASTING LADY, Skinner and GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE are watching as LUNCHLADY DORIS reads with an auditioning NELSON. LUNCHLADY DORIS (BORED) At last the world is safe, eh, Fallout Boy? NELSON Watch ou... DIRECTOR (DISMISSIVE) Next. NELSON Aw, crap. Nelson exits past ANOTHER BOY. LUNCHLADY DORIS At last the world is safe, eh, Fallout Boy? ANOTHER BOY

Watch out radio! Active Man!

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 16. SCENE_3 Grd DIRECTOR Next. ANOTHER BOY Thanks for nothing... less than a great opportunity. He exits. ON THE DIRECTOR DIRECTOR We’re never gonna find... wait a minute. He sees RALPH standing in the corner. DIRECTOR (CONT’D) That child has the exact qualities we’ve been looking for. He’s perfect. What’s his name? CASTING LADY I don’t know. He just came along with one of the others. He didn’t sign up officially. DIRECTOR Forget him, then. It wouldn’t be fair to the other children who filled out their application forms in full. Everyone nods. DIRECTOR Next!

Bart enters confidently.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 17. ScENE 3 ConT'D LUNCHLADY DORIS SS At last the world is safe, eh, Fallout Boy? BART

(SUPER BRILLIANT READING) Watch out,

PRODUCER Brilliant reading. Again!

BART

PRODUCER Fantastic! One more time.

BART

Watch out, Radioactive Man! PRODUCER Congratulations, Bart Simpson, you’re our new Fallout Boy. That’s what I’d be saying to you if you weren’t an inch too short. Next. Bart looks crushed.

FADE OUT:

END OF ACT ONE

CT TWO

FADE IN:

SCENE 4

EXT. SIMPSON BACKYARD - DAY

We see Homer in a hammock eating rapidly from a bucket of chicken.

BART (V.0O.) Eat faster, Dad! Faster! WIDEN to see Bart’s arms locked around a tree supporting one end of the Hammock holding Homer. (His legs hold the hammock net.) BART (CONT’D) I need to grow an inch before tomorrow or I won’t get that part. HOMER Well, if it’s for the arts. Homer begins eating even faster. EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - ESTABLISHING - NEXT MORNING INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN Bart races into the kitchen and excitedly measures himself against pencil marks on the wall. The marks are wiggly, just like his hair. The rest of the family is eating breakfast. BART (MOAN) I only grew half an inch. I’m still too short. MARGE Growing half an inch in one day is

still pretty good, Bart.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94

Page 19.

SCENE 4 CONT'D

LISA as

Yeah, that’s how fast Grampa is shrinking.

We see the top of GRAMPA’S head poke over the table as he walks by.

GRAMPA I’m as tall as I ever was. BART Is there anyway I can look taller without actually being taller? EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE Bart walks out of the house wearing vertical striped clothes and platform shoes. His hair is combed straight up, and he’s walking a VERY TINY DOG. EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - ESTABLISHING INT. SKINNER’S OFFICE

Bart walks into Skinner’s office. Milhouse is there, auditioning.

BART Good news, gentlemen. I’ve grown that extra inch you wanted, plus several feet more. DIRECTOR (DECISIVELY) We have found our new Fallout Boy! And he’s right over there! He points to a stunned Milhouse. Bart also looks stunned, then walks over in front of Milhouse so the finger is pointing at him. He looks hopeful. The director shifts

his position so he’s pointing at Milhouse again. Bart looks stricken.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 20. SCENE 4 CxTID EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY ———

Milhouse exits the school and is greeted by a CHEERING crowd, passing Bart.

CROWD Milhouse! Hey Fallout Boy! Over Here! Milhouse looks bewildered. All the GIRLS are ogling hin, suddenly lovestruck. HUTZ pushes his way through the crowd, passing Bart. LIONEL HUTZ Outta the way, boy. Milhouse, baby! Lionel Hutz, your new agent, manager, bodyguard and drug dealer. (OFF MILHOUSE’S LOOK) Keeper awayer. A TAILOR rushes up. TAILOR Mr. Milhouse, you’1l be needing a new wardrobe. (PROUDLY) Feel the material in this propeller hat. EXT. MILHOUSE’S HOUSE

Milhouse runs up to his house, just a few steps ahead of a mob of pursuing HANGERS-ON.

MILHOUSE Leave me alone! (CALLING) Mom! Dad! Make them stop! Milhouse rushes into the house. INT. LIVING ROOM

Milhouse’s PARENTS are unpacking boxes full of stereos, big screen TVs etc.

MILHOUSE What is all this stuff?

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 21. SCENE_4 ONTD MR. VAN HOUTEN We heard you’ve become a star. We decided we’d better start living in the fast lane. MILHOUSE What if I’m not a success? How will you pay for all this? MRS. VAN HOUTEN I’m sorry, I can’t hear you son. I’m wearing a Jacuzzi suit.

She’s wearing a clear plastic suit full of foaming, BUBBLING water. Milhouse looks dismayed.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - BART’S BEDROOM Bart lies depressed on his bed. BART (MISERABLE) I just missed out on the greatest opportunity of my entire life. George Burns was right, show business is a hideous bitch goddess. LISA Well, at least your best friend got the part. BART

What good does that do me?

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 22. SCENE 4 QUTD

LISA Fe a Don’t worry, Bart. This doesn’t leave you out in the cold. Milhouse is going to need an entourage... hangers on, butt-licks, coattail surfers...

BART You‘re right. Why should I settle for less than second best?

LISA You can have first crack at his half- eaten deli platters. You can watch big screen TV in his room when he has guests he doesn’t want you to meet. You can use his Palm Springs getaway in August.

BART You’re right, Lis. The future’s never looked brighter. I’11 save you a black banana.

They hug. Suddenly some TEAMSTERS begin reeling movie lights into the room. Homer pops his head in.

HOMER Guess what, kids. They’re gonna pay us five hundred dollars a day to film some of the movie here. (LOW) They were going to do it at Flanders’ house, but I filled them in about his cannibal

past.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 23.

SCENE 4 CONT'D

BART

What cannibal past? HOMER That past he had. You know. Homer sees the director and walks up to hin. HOMER (CONT’D) (AWESTRUCK) Hey, didn’t you direct "Three Times Eternity"? DIRECTOR Yes I did. Homer holds his nose and fans. HOMER (IT STINKS) Hooey! PRODUCER I’ve got an idea, Mr. Simpson. Why don’t you get something to eat from our food truck? Homer seems to vaporize, leaving a residual image. PRODUCER (CONT’D) Mr. Simpson? INT. PRODUCER’S TRAILER SCENE GB KRUSTY BURSTS in. He has a copy of the script in his hand. KRUSTY You’ve got to give me the part of Krispy the Klown. DIRECTOR

I’m sorry, you’re wrong for the part.

KRUSTY

But look at my range!

cU - COMPOSITE PHOTO The photos show Krusty 1) frowning, 2) laughing, 3) wearing a sailor’s hat, and 4) studiously looking through a microscope. All his poses show him in full clown makeup. BACK TO SCENE The producer hands back the photo. PRODUCER Very nice. But we’ve already given the part to Robert Vaughn. They walk off. KRUSTY (TO HIMSELF) Why does Robert Vaughn always get the clown parts?

Bart wanders around, looking at all the movie-related activity that’s going on.

Up and ATOM.

A group of MOVIE PEOPLE start RUNNING angrily toward Bart. BART (CONT’D) I didn’t do it! I wished him well! I wished him weeelll!

Bart runs up and sees it is actually a MIDGET dressed like Milhouse, who is pinned under a flashing x-ray machine.

BART (CONT’D) Hey, you’re not Milhouse.

MIDGET No. I am just Milhouse when he gets hurt.

The director walks up.

DIRECTOR (CALLING) Okay, let’s get the real Milhouse over here under the x-ray truck.

MILHOUSE

(MISERABLE) Hi, Bart.

Radioactive Man rushes up to Milhouse, lifts the truck and the flashing x-ray machine off him and helps him up.

RADIOACTIVE MAN Up and AT THEM!

FALLOUT BOY (MILHOUSE) Thanks for the help, Mysterious Stranger. Say, I think those x-rays gave me super powers.

PROP GUY Cows don’t look like cows on film. You gotta use horses. RALPH What do you do if you want something that looks like a horse? PROP GUY Usually we just tape a bunch of cats together. ON HOMER

HOMER You guys work on the movie? TEAMSTER You saying we’re not working? HOMER I always wanted to be a teamster. (ADMIRINGLY) So lazy and surly. Mind if I relax next to you? He stretches out next to them. They see he’s as

ON QUIMBY AND WIGGUM SCENE ¢ ae

They walk up to the producer. They don’t look too sure of themselves.

PRODUCER (TIRED) What is it now, Quimby? QUIMBY Nothing. Nothing. (BEAT) Only... uh... the city has just passed another movie film tax. I/m sorry. PRODUCER (VERY TIRED) How much do we owe you? Quimby and Wiggum look at each other, surprised. QUIMBY I’m not sure. WIGGUM To tell you the truth, we didn’t think you'd go for it. INT. MILHOUSE’S STARWAGON Bart looks around in awe. BART Wow. You’ve really got it made now, Milhouse. This is living. MILHOUSE Is it, Bart? Is it? BART Yes.

Bart looks at the Oscar on one of the shelves.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 30.

SCENE 6 conT'D

BART (CONT’D)

Cool! An Oscar! (READING INSCRIPTION) "To Milhouse Van Houten, in anticipation of a lifetime of achievement."

MILHOUSE Ever since I became a movie star, I’ve been miserable. I had to get up at five in the morning just for makeup.

He looks at himself in the mirror.

MILHOUSE (CONT’D) I like the way the blush accentuates my cheekbones, but it’s not worth it. And making movies is so horribly repetitive. I’ve said "jiminy jillikers" so many times the words have lost all meaning.

The director sticks his head in the door.

DIRECTOR We've got to do the "jiminy jillikers" scene again, Milhouse.

MILHOUSE (LOW) But we already did it. It took

seven hours, but we did it. It’s done.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 31. SCENE 6 can't’ DIRECTOR —_—_— Yeah, but we got to do it from different angles. Again and again and again and again and again. MILHOUSE (WAIL) Ahhhhh! BART (OBLIVIOUS) Yeah. (SATISFIED) Ahhhh! (THEN NOTICING THE COLD CUTS) What do we have here? Salami... pimento loaf... INT. EDITING TRAILER ON THE SCREEN

The evil Krispy the Klown has imprisoned Radioactive Man and Fallout Boy.

KRISPY I’ve got you now, Radioactive Man. (AFTERTHOUGHT) Oh, and you too, Fallout Boy.

FALLOUT BOY Jiminy jillikers!

RADIOACTIVE MAN There’s no need for profanity, Fallout Boy.

We PULL OUT to see the director and producer watching dailies.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 32. SCENE 6 ContT'D PRODUCER ee Everyone in town has been gouging us silly, but it’s worth it. It’s all up there on the screen. DIRECTOR Yes, and that Milhouse is going to be big. Gabby Hayes big. INT. POWER PLANT

Everyone is preparing for a complicated action scene as Bart watches.

DIRECTOR (INTO MEGAPHONE) Okay, listen up, everybody. This is the hardest, most expensive scene in the movie and we only get one shot at it so we have to do it right. Fallout Boy will rescue Radioactive Man moments before he’s hit with a forty- foot wall of sulfuric acid that will horribly burn everything in its path. So I want to see goggles, people. Okay... Cue the mile-long domino chain! Hatch the Doomsday eggs! Tip the acid vats... now! And, roll filn! ON RADIOACTIVE MAN He is tied up with glowing radioactive rods. RADIOACTIVE MAN

Only Fallout Boy can save me now.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 33.

ScEn€ 6 Contd He waits for a few beats. His eyes dart off camera for a moment. DIRECTOR Cue Fallout Boy!... Fallout Boy? RADIOACTIVE MAN’S POV A huge wave of sulfuric acid heads for him. Radioactive Man stoically puts his goggles on. The wall of acid SWEEPS him away. RADIOACTIVE MAN (CONT’D) (DOPPLER EFFECT) I/11 be in my trailer!

He is promptly driven like a spike halfway into the wall of his trailer.

RADIO ACTIVE MAN (CONT’D) Somebody hose me down. My legs are tingling... make that burning. ON THE DIRECTOR AND THE PRODUCER They look at each other with dismay. DIRECTOR (FURIOUS) Where the hell is Milhouse?! Lunchlady Doris runs into the room. LUNCHLADY DORIS Hold on to your hats. Milhouse has disappeared! Everyone, including Bart, looks stunned. The producer and director survey their destroyed, dissolving sets. (Clock melted a la Salvador Dali painting.) PRODUCER (QUIETLY) That one shot cost us a

million dollars.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 34. SCENE © CONTID DIRECTOR ee (FATALISTIC) Oh well. At least we didn’t blow our payroll office. A nearby STAGEHAND hears this. STAGEHAND #2 (YELLING) Blow the payroll office!

We PAN over to a payroll trailer, which EXPLODES. Money rains down on the producer and director, who look sad.

FADE OUT:

ACT THREE FADE IN: SCENE 7

pees Maes CU - NEWSPAPER

Close up of a spinning newspaper, headline says: "Milhouse Disappears! - Movie On Hold." Spinning Variety: "Milhouse Meltdown! Ankles Pic, Flick Sick." Another spinning newspaper reads: "Spinning Newspaper Injures Printer."

EXT. SPRINGFIELD STREET

People are searching everywhere, calling "MILHOUSE." A producer is tacking up a reward poster for Milhouse.

cU - POSTER

It says: "Milhouse! $8,000,000 Reward! Dead Or Alive!" The "dead" has been hastily crossed out.

EXT. SHOOT LOCATION

The whole area is being scoured for signs of Milhouse. A group of people tip over Milhouse’s StarWagon.

MOVIE GUY He’s not under here. 2ND MOVIE GUY Let’s tip over the school. They hurry off. EXT. OUTDOOR RESTAURANT IN PARIS

Wiggum, EDDIE and LOU are drinking champagne and looking out at the Eiffel Tower.

EDDIE It was a good idea checking for Milhouse in Paris, Chief.

WIGGUM Yeah. Let’s see if he’s at any of the

strip clubs.

The producer and director are watching a scene the EDITOR has put together from existing scenes.

EDITOR (SMUG) Thanks to modern editing techniques, we can use existing footage to complete the film without Milhouse. Watch. ON THE SCREEN

Radioactive Man is standing in a large cave confronted by a bunch of TOUGHS. Fallout Boy isn’t there.

RADIOACTIVE MAN (SIDE OF HIS MOUTH) Looks like we’re in trouble, Fallout Boy.

The scene changes to Radioactive Man standing next to Fallout Boy in a meadow.

FALLOUT BOY (MILHOUSE) Jiminy Jillikers, Radioactive Man! What’11 we do? The scene changes back to Radioactive Man in the cave. RADIOACTIVE MAN We’ll have to fight our way out. Are you ready? The scene changes to Fallout Boy sitting on a couch. FALLOUT BOY (MILHOUSE) Yes.

The scene changes to Radioactive Man and Fallout Boy fist FIGHTING with CENTIPEDE MEN on the moon.

The editor looks at the producer smugly. PRODUCER You’re fired. The editor smugly opens a newspaper. EDITOR (SMUGLY READING) "Help wanted."

WIGGUM

Uh... yeah, I did kind of trail off,

didn’t I? EXT. SIMPSON FRONT YARD

Homer sits in a beach chair with a fishing rod. The line disappears over a hedge.

HOMER Heh heh heh. To catch a Milhouse, you have to think like a Milhouse.

We CUT TO the end of Homer’s line and see it is attached to a pair of thick Milhouse-style glasses.

BACK TO SCENE MARGE You’re never going to get him this way. Suddenly, the line goes taut, the rod bends, and the reel starts SPINNING wildly. Homer struggles. Marge braces her arms around him, steadying his position. MARGE Play him! Give him some line! Homer soon reels in the Midget, wearing the glasses. MIDGET I knew free glasses were too good to be true. He enters a building with a sign that reads "Slot Car Heaven: 124th the size, 3/8ths the fun."

The Comic Book Guy races OTTO, who has a miniature bus. BART Have you seen Milhouse? orto No. Now go away. We’re racing for the title of the Champion of the Universe. EXT. ABANDONED FACTORY We see an old delapidated factory with shattered windows, cobwebs, etc. A sign reads: "Spirograph Factory." (Some letters have fallen off the second word, but somehow it still spells "Factory," though the letters are oddly spaced.)

INT. FACTORY

We see a slightly crazed-looking MAN in a lab coat endlessly making spirograph drawings.

BART Have you seen Milhouse? SPIROGRAPH MAN No. BART Okay, thanks. Bart begins to leave. SPIROGRAPH MAN Wait.

Bart turns, hopefully.

SCENE 7 CONT'D

SPIROGRAPH MAN (CONT’D) Did you know there’s a direct correlation between the decline of Spirographs and the rise in gang activity? Think about it. BART I will. SPIROGRAPH MAN (BITTER) No you won’t He goes back to his spirograph. EXT. OLD MINE SHAFT

A sign reads: "Dangerous Abandoned Mine--Children Under Six Proceed With Extreme Caution."

INT. MINE SHAFT

Bart has ridden a cart down into the mine. He has a flashlight taped to his baseball cap.

BART Have you seen Milhouse? We see he’s talking to a race of big-eyed, transluscent, lightly-glowing CHILDREN. (We can see their hearts beating.) They shrink and blink in the bright light, and shake their heads grimly. BART Okay, thanks. Bart starts to leave. CAVEPERSON Come back with vitamin Cc.

EXT. MINE SHAFT

Bart emerges, suddenly has an epiphany, and SNAPS his fingers.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 41.

SCENE TONT'D BART Wait, I know where Milhouse is! EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - ESTABLISHING - BART’S TREEHOUSE QHIE 8

INT. TREEHOUSE - BART’S POV

Bart scans the walls of the treehouse.

BART Oh, I guess I don’t know where

Milhouse... Oh, there he is.

We see that Milhouse is in the one corner Bart hadn’t

looked.

MILHOUSE Stay away from me, Bart. I’m not going back. I’m not cut out to be a star.

BART But Milhouse, being a star is every patriotic American’s dream.

MILHOUSE Not mine. It’s a sham, Bart. You get up on that big screen pretending to be a hero, but you’re not. The real heroes are out there, toiling day and night on more important things.

BART (NODDING SAGELY) Television.

MILHOUSE No! Curing heart disease and wiping

out world hunger.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 42.

SCENE 8 contd

BART But they haven’t cured anything. Heart disease and world hunger are still rampant. Those do-gooders are all a bunch of well meaning but pitiful losers. Every last one of them. Want results? You have to go to the Schwarzeneggers, the Stallones, and, to a lesser extent, the Van Damnes. Suddenly the tranquility is shattered by the sound of WHIRRING helicopter blades. We see a silhouetted figure hanging from a rope. He jumps gracefully into the treehouse. The light hits him and we see it’s... BART/MILHOUSE Mickey Rooney! MICKEY ROONEY (VERY OUT OF BREATH) Hi Milhouse. The studio sent me to talk to you, being a former child star myself and the number one box office draw from 1939 through 1940. BART Wow, spanning two decades. MILHOUSE How’d you find us? MICKEY ROONEY

They tapped your treehouse phone.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 43.

SCENE 8 ONTD We see the classic two paper cups on a string with an obvious second string coming out of the middle. (We follow it out of the treehouse and down to an incredibly sophisticated surveillance van jammed with electronics. We see the producer and director huddled around another paper cup on a string.) BACK TO TREEHOUSE MICKEY ROONEY Milhouse, you can’t quit this movie. I’ve seen your work. It’s good, very good. Van Johnson good. MILHOUSE I know I’m good. Movie stardom is just so hollow. MICKEY ROONEY Hollow? The only thing in show business that’s hollow is the music industry. C’mon, Milhouse, you have to do this. If not for yourself, then for the movie-going public, and for the foreign markets, more important than ever nowadays... and finally, for me, the Mickster.

MILHOUSE

MICKEY ROONEY (SIGH) All right, I tried.

He turns and puts a hand on Bart’s shoulder.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 44.

SCENE 8 CONT'D

MICKEY ROONEY (CONT’D) (SLYLY TO BART) Fortunately we have a perfectly good Fallout Boy right here. Bart looks extremely hopeful. SMASH CUT TO: Mickey Rooney, made up to look like Milhouse as Fallout Boy, capers energetically in front of the stunned producer and director. MICKEY ROONEY (SELLING IT) Jiminy jillikers, jiminy jillikers, jiminy jillikers! The producer and director look at each other. DIRECTOR We’re shutting down production. PRODUCER We don’t have any money left anyway. QUIMBY There’s, uh, a hundred thousand dollar leaving town tax. EXT. STREET

The townspeople watch sadly as the caravan of movie people drive off. Mickey addresses the town sadly.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 45.

SCENE 8 ConTD

MICKEY Well, I hope you’re all satisfied. You’ve bankrupted a bunch of naive movie folks. They’re from Hollywood, where values are different. They weren’t thinking about the money. They just wanted to tell a story. A story about a radioactive man. And you slick small towners took them for all they were worth. The townspeople hang their heads. oTTto Should we give them some of their money back? QUIMBY No. A helicopter SWOOPS down and lowers a ladder for Mickey. HELICOPTER PILOT (0.S.) (THROUGH MEGAPHONE) Hurry, Mr. Rooney! We’ve got a disenchanted little girl in a jello pudding commercial. MICKEY ROONEY I can play that.

Mickey grabs the ladder, and they fly off into the distance.

2F17 RECORD 9/12/94 Page 46.

SCENE 8 CoNT'D

EXT. HOLLYWOOD AND VINE We can see the "Hollywood" sign on the hills in the background. The Winnebagos pull to a stop in front of a large CROWD of caring Hollywood people who are holding signs that say "Welcome Troubled Production" etc.

The producer and director get out of their Winnebago and are hugged by members of the caring crowd.

SHALLOW LOOKING HOLLYWOODITE We know you don’t have any money left, but that doesn’t matter. Just take whatever you need from our boutiques until you get back on your feet. PRODUCER Thank God we’re back in Hollywood, where people treat each other right. We PULL BACK on the idyllic heart-warming scene of a beautifully clean, friendly Hollywood with lots of flowers {people waving "hello" to each other, etc.) as "Lean On Me" begins to PLAY. FADE OUT:

THE END
